message:
chore: sync concurrent agent changes

- .beads/issues.jsonl: beads status updates
- src/distributed/recovery.rs: recovery improvements
- src/plan/mod.rs: plan module adjustments
- src/raptorq/mod.rs: raptorq module updates
- src/raptorq/rfc6330.rs: new RFC 6330 implementation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3rhs","title":"Platform reactors E2E test suite: cross-platform I/O verification","description":"# Platform Reactors E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive test suite verifying reactor implementations work correctly on
all supported platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows).

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/reactor/
├── mod.rs                    # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                # Shared test utilities
│   └── tcp_echo.rs           # TCP echo server helper
├── trait_contract/
│   ├── register.rs           # Registration tests
│   ├── poll.rs               # Polling tests
│   ├── wake.rs               # Wakeup tests
│   └── lifecycle.rs          # Cleanup tests
├── tcp/
│   ├── connect.rs            # TCP connect tests
│   ├── accept.rs             # TCP accept tests
│   └── throughput.rs         # Throughput benchmarks
├── stress/
│   ├── many_connections.rs   # High connection count
│   ├── rapid_register.rs     # Fast register/deregister
│   └── concurrent_poll.rs    # Multi-threaded polling
└── platform_specific/
    ├── io_uring.rs           # Linux io_uring specific
    ├── epoll.rs              # Linux epoll specific
    ├── kqueue.rs             # macOS specific
    └── iocp.rs               # Windows specific
```

## Test Categories

### 1. Trait Contract Tests
Verify all reactors implement the Reactor trait correctly:
- register() adds source to reactor
- reregister() modifies interest
- deregister() removes source
- poll() returns ready events
- wake() interrupts blocked poll()

### 2. TCP Integration Tests
Real TCP operations through reactor:
- Client connect (non-blocking)
- Server accept (listener)
- Read/write cycles
- Concurrent connections

### 3. Stress Tests
- 10K+ connections simultaneously
- Rapid register/deregister cycling
- Multi-threaded poll() calls
- Memory pressure scenarios

### 4. Platform-Specific Tests
Tests that only run on specific platforms:
- io_uring: sqpoll, registered buffers
- epoll: EPOLLET edge-triggered behavior
- kqueue: kevent batching
- IOCP: overlapped I/O completion

## CI Matrix

### GitHub Actions Configuration
```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    os: [ubuntu-latest, ubuntu-20.04, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    rust: [stable, nightly]
    include:
      - os: ubuntu-latest
        features: io-uring
      - os: ubuntu-20.04
        features: epoll-fallback  # Older kernel without io_uring
```

## Logging Requirements

### Log Fields
```rust
tracing::info!(
    reactor_type = %reactor_name,
    platform = %std::env::consts::OS,
    connection_count = count,
    poll_latency_us = latency.as_micros(),
    events_per_poll = events,
    "Reactor test metrics"
);
```

### Artifact Collection
- Per-test timing data
- Event histograms
- Memory usage snapshots
- Platform-specific diagnostics

## Test Scripts

### Run All Platform Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export RUST_LOG="asupersync::reactor=debug,test=info"

echo "=== Platform Reactor Tests ==="
echo "Platform: $(uname -s)"
echo "Kernel: $(uname -r)"

# Detect available reactor
if [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    if [[ $(uname -r | cut -d. -f1) -ge 5 ]]; then
        echo "Reactor: io_uring (preferred)"
    else
        echo "Reactor: epoll (fallback)"
    fi
elif [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    echo "Reactor: kqueue"
fi

cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_reactor -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee reactor_tests.log
```

## Dependencies
- Requires: All reactor implementations (bd-2m9k, bd-2q5d, bd-39vt, bd-1gx6)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All trait contract tests pass on all platforms
- [ ] TCP integration tests work
- [ ] Stress tests pass (10K+ connections)
- [ ] CI runs on Linux, macOS, Windows
- [ ] Structured logging with metrics
- [ ] Test scripts documented
- [ ] Platform-specific tests run only where applicable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:22:36.013725924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:36.219120378Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:36.219041922Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","platform"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rhs","depends_on_id":"bd-1gx6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhs","depends_on_id":"bd-2m9k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhs","depends_on_id":"bd-2q5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhs","depends_on_id":"bd-2un5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhs","depends_on_id":"bd-39vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3rih","title":"[ALIEN][SCHED][LYAP] Efficient RuntimeSnapshot extraction (no hot-path allocs)","description":"## Purpose

The governor needs a snapshot of Σ (runtime state) to compute V(Σ).

We must compute this cheaply:
- no per-poll allocations
- stable deterministic counts

## Snapshot contents (candidate)

- outstanding obligations by kind
- pending cancel requests / acks
- deadline slack summary
- IO in-flight counts
- ready queue depth signals

## Acceptance criteria

- Snapshot can be computed with bounded work.
- Snapshot values are deterministic under lab runtime.","design":"### Implementation plan

Add a deterministic, allocation-free constructor for the Lyapunov governor snapshot.

**Location:** `src/obligation/lyapunov.rs` (`impl StateSnapshot`), because the type's docs already claim it can be constructed from `RuntimeState`.

### Snapshot extraction semantics (exact)

Given a `RuntimeState` with logical time `now = state.now`:

- `time`: `now`
- `live_tasks`: count of `state.tasks` whose `TaskState` is *not* terminal (`!task.state.is_terminal()`).
- `pending_obligations`: count of `state.obligations` where `obligation.is_pending()` (i.e. `ObligationState::Reserved`).
- `obligation_age_sum_ns`: `sum now.duration_since(obligation.reserved_at)` over pending obligations.
- `draining_regions`: count of `state.regions` with `RegionState` in `{Draining, Finalizing}`.
- `deadline_pressure`: for each **live** task with a deadline in its `CxInner` budget:
  - `slack_ns = deadline.duration_since(now)` (saturating at 0 when overdue)
  - `term = max(0, 1 - slack_ns / D0)`
  - `deadline_pressure = sum term`

`D0` is a fixed normalization constant (chosen as 1s) to make the signal represent "tasks due soon or overdue" rather than far-future deadlines.

### Determinism + performance constraints

- **No allocations on the hot path:** no `Vec`, no `HashMap`, no `collect`.
- **Deterministic iteration:** only scan arenas in their natural order; no hash iteration.
- **Graceful degradation:** if a task's `CxInner` lock is poisoned, skip its deadline contribution (do not panic).

### Follow-ups (if needed)

If scanning overhead becomes noticeable in profiling, add incremental counters (updated on task/obligation/region transitions) so snapshots become O(1).
","acceptance_criteria":"- `StateSnapshot::from_runtime_state(&RuntimeState)` exists and is `#[must_use]`.
- Snapshot extraction is deterministic and allocation-free.
- Unit tests cover:
  - live task counting
  - pending obligation counting + age sum
  - draining/finalizing region counting
  - deadline pressure for both "due soon" and "overdue" cases
- `cargo test -p asupersync snapshot_from_runtime` passes (once repo-wide compile blockers are cleared).
","notes":"### Progress

- Implemented `StateSnapshot::from_runtime_state` + unit tests in `src/obligation/lyapunov.rs`.

### Current blocker

- Repo currently fails to compile tests due to a stale `TraceEventKind::ObligationAcquire` reference in `src/trace/certificate.rs` (should be `ObligationReserve`). I've contacted the current holder of the `src/trace/**` reservation to patch/release.
","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperPeak","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:12:08.269050877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:21:59.985741483Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rih","depends_on_id":"bd-12rg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rih","depends_on_id":"bd-1ikk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3rih","title":"[ALIEN][SCHED][LYAP] Efficient RuntimeSnapshot extraction (no hot-path allocs)","description":"## Purpose

The governor needs a snapshot of Σ (runtime state) to compute V(Σ).

We must compute this cheaply:
- no per-poll allocations
- stable deterministic counts

## Snapshot contents (candidate)

- outstanding obligations by kind
- pending cancel requests / acks
- deadline slack summary
- IO in-flight counts
- ready queue depth signals

## Acceptance criteria

- Snapshot can be computed with bounded work.
- Snapshot values are deterministic under lab runtime.","design":"### Implementation plan

Add a deterministic, allocation-free constructor for the Lyapunov governor snapshot.

**Location:** `src/obligation/lyapunov.rs` (`impl StateSnapshot`), because the type's docs already claim it can be constructed from `RuntimeState`.

### Snapshot extraction semantics (exact)

Given a `RuntimeState` with logical time `now = state.now`:

- `time`: `now`
- `live_tasks`: count of `state.tasks` whose `TaskState` is *not* terminal (`!task.state.is_terminal()`).
- `pending_obligations`: count of `state.obligations` where `obligation.is_pending()` (i.e. `ObligationState::Reserved`).
- `obligation_age_sum_ns`: `sum now.duration_since(obligation.reserved_at)` over pending obligations.
- `draining_regions`: count of `state.regions` with `RegionState` in `{Draining, Finalizing}`.
- `deadline_pressure`: for each **live** task with a deadline in its `CxInner` budget:
  - `slack_ns = deadline.duration_since(now)` (saturating at 0 when overdue)
  - `term = max(0, 1 - slack_ns / D0)`
  - `deadline_pressure = sum term`

`D0` is a fixed normalization constant (chosen as 1s) to make the signal represent "tasks due soon or overdue" rather than far-future deadlines.

### Determinism + performance constraints

- **No allocations on the hot path:** no `Vec`, no `HashMap`, no `collect`.
- **Deterministic iteration:** only scan arenas in their natural order; no hash iteration.
- **Graceful degradation:** if a task's `CxInner` lock is poisoned, skip its deadline contribution (do not panic).

### Follow-ups (if needed)

If scanning overhead becomes noticeable in profiling, add incremental counters (updated on task/obligation/region transitions) so snapshots become O(1).
","acceptance_criteria":"- `StateSnapshot::from_runtime_state(&RuntimeState)` exists and is `#[must_use]`.
- Snapshot extraction is deterministic and allocation-free.
- Unit tests cover:
  - live task counting
  - pending obligation counting + age sum
  - draining/finalizing region counting
  - deadline pressure for both "due soon" and "overdue" cases
- `cargo test -p asupersync snapshot_from_runtime` passes (once repo-wide compile blockers are cleared).
","notes":"### Progress

- Implemented `StateSnapshot::from_runtime_state` + unit tests in `src/obligation/lyapunov.rs`.

### Current blocker

- Repo currently fails to compile tests due to a stale `TraceEventKind::ObligationAcquire` reference in `src/trace/certificate.rs` (should be `ObligationReserve`). I've contacted the current holder of the `src/trace/**` reservation to patch/release.
","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperPeak","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:12:08.269050877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:48:24.093529903Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rih","depends_on_id":"bd-12rg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rih","depends_on_id":"bd-1ikk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests assert both:
  - optimized plan structure
  - certificate verifies","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:16:57.039901031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:15:12.421816710Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:15:12.421516962Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","plan","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rjp","depends_on_id":"bd-2de4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:54:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rpb","title":"Fix unsafe impl Send in pool.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:09.216873525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:22.983852136Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:22.983801701Z","close_reason":"Fixed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3rq0","title":"Parker: fix lost wakeup race condition","description":"# Critical Bug: Parker Lost Wakeup Race Condition

## Location
`src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs:268-294`

## Vulnerability Description

The `Parker` implementation has a classic lost wakeup vulnerability:

```rust
pub fn park(&self) {
    let (lock, cvar) = &*self.inner;
    let mut notified = lock.lock().unwrap();
    while !*notified {                    // Check notified
        notified = cvar.wait(notified).unwrap();  // Wait
    }
    *notified = false;
}
```

## Race Condition Timeline

```
Worker A (parking):           Worker B (unparking):
─────────────────             ───────────────────
lock.lock()
*notified == false
                              lock.lock() blocks
                              ...waiting...
cvar.wait() releases lock
                              lock acquired
                              *notified = true
                              cvar.notify_one()
                              lock released
cvar.wait() reacquires
*notified == true
returns (OK)
```

This case works. But what about:

```
Worker A (parking):           Worker B (unparking):
─────────────────             ───────────────────
lock.lock()
check *notified == false
                              unpark() called
                              lock.lock() acquired
                              *notified = true
                              cvar.notify_one() - NOBODY WAITING
                              lock released
cvar.wait() starts waiting
... FOREVER (notification lost)
```

## Impact

- **Severity**: CRITICAL (workers can permanently hang)
- **Exploitability**: Triggered by timing, more likely under load
- **Impact**: Tasks never executed, runtime appears hung

## Root Cause

The window between checking `*notified` and entering `cvar.wait()` allows the 
notification to be consumed before the wait begins.

## Proposed Fix

The standard fix is to re-check the condition after acquiring the lock 
but BEFORE waiting. The current code does this with `while !*notified`, 
but the issue is that `cvar.notify_one()` can fire when no one is waiting.

Better approach - use atomics with the condvar:

```rust
pub struct Parker {
    notified: AtomicBool,
    mutex: Mutex<()>,
    condvar: Condvar,
}

pub fn park(&self) {
    // Fast path - already notified
    if self.notified.swap(false, Ordering::Acquire) {
        return;
    }
    
    let guard = self.mutex.lock().unwrap();
    // Double-check after acquiring lock
    while !self.notified.swap(false, Ordering::Acquire) {
        guard = self.condvar.wait(guard).unwrap();
    }
}

pub fn unpark(&self) {
    self.notified.store(true, Ordering::Release);
    // Always notify to handle race
    let _guard = self.mutex.lock().unwrap();
    self.condvar.notify_one();
}
```

The key insight: `notified` is atomic AND condvar-protected, so we can 
check it without holding the lock (fast path) but still use condvar 
for efficient waiting.

## Alternative: crossbeam Parker

Consider using `crossbeam::sync::Parker` which handles this correctly:
https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.Parker.html

## Testing Strategy

1. Stress test with rapid park/unpark cycles
2. Loom test for systematic interleaving
3. Add tracing to detect stuck workers
4. Verify with high worker count (many parkers)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Race condition eliminated
- [ ] Fast path for already-notified case
- [ ] Loom test passing
- [ ] Stress test with 1000+ park/unpark cycles
- [ ] No performance regression","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-31T20:56:52.492812273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T21:19:39.265413381Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T21:19:39.265333443Z","close_reason":"Fixed Parker wakeup race; tests passed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rq0","depends_on_id":"bd-e71n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
